---
title: "Security"
date: "2026-04-24"
order: 10
fig-cap-location: bottom
categories: [ccde-written]
---

```{=html}
<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Nunito:wght@400;600;700&family=PT+Sans:wght@400;700&display=swap');
body { font-family: 'PT Sans', sans-serif; }
h1, h2, h3, h4 { font-family: 'Nunito', sans-serif; }
figcaption { text-align: center; }
</style>
```

## Overview

Designing a modern network that is secure is one of the most complicated tasks that network designers confront. Mobility and communication over the Internet are becoming one of the primary methods of communications and are essential requirements for many businesses. To address these trends, sophisticated security countermeasures are required. A good security design follows a structured approach that divides the network into domains and applies security in layers — this is known as **defense in depth**, where multiple layers of protection are strategically located across the network so that a failure of one layer does not leave the network at risk.

::: {#fig-layered-security}
```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center">
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:721px;" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" version="1.1" width="793px" viewBox="-0.5 -0.5 793 721"><defs/><g><g data-cell-id="0"><g data-cell-id="1"><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-14"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="400" y="350" width="270" height="270" rx="16.2" ry="16.2" fill="#f5f5f5" stroke="#666666" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(245, 245, 245); stroke: rgb(102, 102, 102);"/></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-1"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="0" width="220" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="28">Protection and inspection at</text><text x="166" y="47">applications layer with more</text><text x="166" y="66">specialized functions</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-2"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="210" width="220" height="120" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="248.5">Network-based solution</text><text x="166" y="267.5">between physical or logical</text><text x="166" y="286.5">domains or networks</text><text x="166" y="305.5">(at the chokepoints)</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-3"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="100" width="220" height="90" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="123.5">Technologies running on the</text><text x="166" y="142.5">end system to protect the</text><text x="166" y="161.5">operating system, file system,</text><text x="166" y="180.5">and applications</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-4"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="350" width="220" height="90" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="373.5">For advanced network security,</text><text x="166" y="392.5">network-based deep packet</text><text x="166" y="411.5">inspection can be introduced</text><text x="166" y="430.5">(signature- or anomaly-based)</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-5"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="460" width="220" height="150" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="485">To protect against DoS type of</text><text x="166" y="504">attacks, traffic control (who can</text><text x="166" y="523">access what and how much</text><text x="166" y="542">bandwidth can be used) also</text><text x="166" y="561">control what device can connect</text><text x="166" y="580">and communicate across the</text><text x="166" y="599">network</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-6"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="400" y="0" width="270" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="535" y="28">Proxy systems, web proxy/filtering,</text><text x="535" y="47">email proxy/filtering,</text><text x="535" y="66">web application firewall (WAF)</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-7"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="160" y="630" width="220" height="90" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="166" y="653.5">To maintain security at the</text><text x="166" y="672.5">physical level where only</text><text x="166" y="691.5">authorized people permitted to</text><text x="166" y="710.5">have physical access</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-8"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="400" y="100" width="270" height="90" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="535" y="123.5">Host-based firewalls,</text><text x="535" y="142.5">host-based IPS,</text><text x="535" y="161.5">file system integrity checking,</text><text x="535" y="180.5">host antivirus/anti-spyware</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-9"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="400" y="210" width="270" height="120" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g fill="#000000" font-family="Garamond" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16px" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><text x="535" y="239">Router with iACL or with traffic</text><text x="535" y="258">inspection capabilities such as:</text><text x="535" y="277">Zone based Firewall (ZBFW),</text><text x="535" y="296">stateless and stateful firewall,</text><text x="535" y="315">next generation firewall (NGNFW)</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-10"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="420" y="370" width="230" height="40" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="420" y="460" width="230" height="60" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); 255));"><text x="535" y="487.5">Layer 3: L3 iACL, L3 routing</text><text x="535" y="506.5">security, CoPP</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-12"><g system,</text><text x="535" y="691.5">CCTV</text></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="Ptw68NtBQNoohyIQZFK3-16"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><path d="M 150 0 L 145 0 Q 140 0 140 10 L 140 30 Q 140 40 135 line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: nowrap; ">Confidentiality and Integrity (Cryptography)<div> L2 to L7 crypto such as MACSec, IPSec, SSL</div></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="740" y="320" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="18px" text-anchor="middle">Confidential...</text></switch></g></g></g></g></g></g><switch><g requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"/><a transform="translate(0,-5)" xlink:href="https://www.drawio.com/doc/faq/svg-export-text-problems" target="_blank"><text text-anchor="middle" font-size="10px" x="50%" y="100%">Text is not SVG - cannot display</text></a></switch></svg>
</div>
```

Elements of Layered Network and Information Security
:::

## Infrastructure Security

This section covers infrastructure security and network firewall considerations, focusing on the integration and impact with regard to network design.

### Device Hardening Techniques

Securing network devices is the first and essential consideration to protect any network. Even with a very secure control plane and network edge design, if one or two network devices are compromised, the network can be taken down easily — for example, by introducing black-holing routes, generating large amounts of malicious traffic from a trusted internal device, or accessing internal zones that are not accessible externally. Additionally, if someone can access a network device, all traffic passing through that device can be sniffed and copied, which is dangerous for organizations handling sensitive information such as credit card numbers or personal data.

To approach device-level protection in a structured manner, a network designer must understand the following types of traffic that network devices normally handle:

- **Transit IP traffic**: Traffic for which a network device makes a typical routing and forwarding decision regarding whether to send the traffic over its interfaces.
- **Receive IP traffic**: Traffic destined to the network node itself, such as toward a router's IP address, and requires CPU processing.
- **Exception IP traffic**: Any IP traffic carrying a nonstandard "exception" attribute.
- **Non-IP traffic**: Typically related to non-IP packets and almost always is not forwarded.

::: {#fig-traffic-types}
```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center">
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:541px;" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" version="1.1" width="681px" viewBox="-0.5 -0.5 681 541"><defs/><g><g data-cell-id="0"><g data-cell-id="1"><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-1"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="0" y="40" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Traffic for which a network device makes a typical routing and forwarding decision regarding<br />whether to send the traffic over its interfaces</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="6" y="85" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Traffic for which a network device makes...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-2"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="350" y="40" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 80px; margin-left: 356px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">A frame that is typically forwarded based on a destination MAC address or any IP packet subject to destination IP address forwarding</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="356" y="85" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">A frame that is typically forwarded based...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-3"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="0" y="180" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 220px; margin-left: 6px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Traffic destined to the network node itself, such as toward a router’s IP address, and requires CPU<br />processing</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="6" y="225" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Traffic destined to the network node itse...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-4"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="350" y="180" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 220px; margin-left: 356px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Traffic belongs to a management plane of the device, such as telnet or SSH, as well as a control plane, such as BGP peering session</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="356" y="225" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Traffic belongs to a management plane of...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-5"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="0" y="320" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 360px; margin-left: 6px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Any IP traffic carrying a nonstandard “exception” attribute</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="6" y="365" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Any IP traffic carrying a nonstandard “exc...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-6"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="350" y="320" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 360px; margin-left: 356px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">A transit IP packet with expired TTL or with IP packet options (for example, requires fragmentation)</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="356" y="365" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">A transit IP packet with expired TTL or w...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-7"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="0" y="460" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 500px; margin-left: 6px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Typically related to non-IP packets and almost always not forwarded</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="6" y="505" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Typically related to non-IP packets and a...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-8"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="350" y="460" width="330" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 324px; height: 1px; padding-top: 500px; margin-left: 356px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">MPLS, IS-IS (CLNP), Layer 2 keepalives, PPP Link Control Protocol (LCP), CDP, IPX</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="356" y="505" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">MPLS, IS-IS (CLNP), Layer 2 keepalives, P...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-9"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="280" y="420" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Non-IP Traffic</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="340" y="440" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Non-IP Traffic</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-10"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="455" y="420" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Receive IP Traffic</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="340" y="160" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Receive IP Traffic</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-16"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="455" y="140" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 155px; margin-left: 456px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; "><span style="font-weight: normal;">Example</span></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="515" y="160" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Example</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-17"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="105" y="140" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 155px; margin-left: 106px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; "><span style="font-weight: normal;">Attributes</span></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="165" y="160" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Attributes</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-18"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="272.5" y="0" width="135" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 133px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 274px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Transit IP Traffic</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="340" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Transit IP Traffic</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-19"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="455" y="0" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 456px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; "><span style="font-weight: normal;">Example</span></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="515" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Example</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="NIPQvwXGMA4REO0Pbhcd-20"><g transform="translate(0.5,0.5)"><rect x="105" y="0" width="120" height="30" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 106px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; "><span style="font-weight: normal;">Attributes</span></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="165" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Attributes</text></switch></g></g></g></g></g></g><switch><g requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"/><a transform="translate(0,-5)" xlink:href="https://www.drawio.com/doc/faq/svg-export-text-problems" target="_blank"><text text-anchor="middle" font-size="10px" x="50%" y="100%">Text is not SVG - cannot display</text></a></switch></svg>
</div>
```

Types of Traffic Handled by Network Devices
:::

Each traffic type maps to a specific device plane, each with its own security considerations, as summarized in @tbl-traffic-planes.

| Device Plane | Security Concern | Example |
|:--|:--|:--|
| Data plane | Responsible for controlling fast-forwarding of traffic passing through a network device. Limited to a certain number of packets per second (PPS) based on hardware throughput. Common protections: iACLs, QoS, RTBH, uRPF. | Transit IP traffic |
| Control plane | Controls and handles all path selection functions. Issues such as a flapping BGP session with a large number of prefixes impact both network stability and device CPU. Common protections: iACLs, routing protection, CoPP. | BGP peering, routing updates |
| Management plane | Relates to management traffic: device access, configuration, troubleshooting, and monitoring. Unauthorized access can lead to device- and network-wide crises. Common protections: CPU/memory thresholding, AAA, CoPP. | SSH, SNMP, Telnet |

: IP Traffic and Device Planes {#tbl-traffic-planes tbl-colwidths="[18,52,30]"}

The following points should be considered as general and foundational guidance to achieving the desired level of device hardening. These points should not conflict with or breach the security policy or company standards.

- **Physical security**: Network devices must be placed physically in a secure location where only authorized people can access them.
- **Authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA)**: Network devices must be accessible only by authorized people. Ideally, access privileges must be multilevel role-based access combined with reliable accounting (logging) to track who has accessed the devices and what has been done.
- **Secure the device management plane**: Management sessions and activities must be performed over protected protocols such as SSH, SSL, SFTP, or IPsec. Out-of-band management access should be provisioned to offer secure, guaranteed direct access to critical network devices during DDoS attacks when in-band management access may be impossible.
- **Protect the hardware resources**: Protecting hardware resources such as CPU is an essential countermeasure. CoPP protects the CPU from spikes caused by excessive control plane traffic. Control plane protection (CPP) also helps protect from exception and transit traffic.
- **Disable unused services**: Unused services should be disabled to limit the chances that an intruder can compromise them to generate malicious traffic or DoS attacks.
- **Disable unused ports**: Unused ports should be disabled and placed into a dedicated VLAN with no traffic allowed. Avoid using the default VLAN 1 or leveraging native VLAN hopping.
- **Monitoring**: Network and device monitoring helps operators optimize device security proactively or reactively. SNMP and NetFlow are the most common protocols used for this purpose.

::: {#fig-device-security-factors}
```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center">
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:441px;" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" version="1.1" width="711px" viewBox="-0.5 -0.5 711 441"><defs/><g><g data-cell-id="0"><g data-cell-id="1"><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-1"><g><rect x="370" y="0" width="340" height="80" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 334px; height: 1px; padding-top: 40px; margin-left: 376px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Normally the organization security policy dictates the security standards for network devices, which must be considered.</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="376" y="45" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">Normally the organization security policy...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-2"><g><rect x="370" y="100" width="340" height="150" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 334px; height: 1px; padding-top: 175px; margin-left: 376px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">The location of the device within the network plays a vital role to specify the potential risks a device needs to be protected against. For instance, a device located in the DMZ performing packet inspection will require high level of protection compared to access switches located within the internal network connecting some endpoints.</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="376" y="180" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">The location of the device within the netw...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-3"><g><rect x="370" y="270" width="340" height="170" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#7ea6e0" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: rgb(126, 166, 224);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 334px; height: 1px; padding-top: 355px; margin-left: 376px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">There might be two network nodes located at the enterprise Internet edge block; one node is a Layer 3 routing node, while the other one performs inspections at the application layer. Based on the role and functional attributes of each device, different hardening standards should be defined here even through both devices are located within the same network segment or logical zone.</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="376" y="360" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px">There might be two network nodes located a...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-13"><g><path d="M 320 40 L 363.63 40" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="stroke" style="stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/><path d="M 368.88 40 L 361.88 43.5 L 363.63 40 L 361.88 36.5 Z" fill="#000000" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-4"><g><path d="M 140 0 L 296 0 L 320 24 L 320 80 L 140 80 L 140 0 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/><path d="M 296 0 L 296 24 L 320 24 Z" fill-opacity="0.05" fill="#000000" stroke="none" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(0, 0, 0);"/><path d="M 296 0 L 296 24 L 320 24" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 178px; height: 1px; padding-top: 40px; margin-left: 141px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; font-variation-settings: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-stretch: normal; line-height: normal; color: rgb(20, 20, 19); text-align: start;">Security Policy</p></div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="230" y="45" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">Organization’s...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-6"><g><path d="M 70.03 90 L 70.03 40.03 L 133.63 40" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="stroke" style="stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/><path d="M 138.88 40 L 131.88 43.5 L 133.63 40 L 131.88 36.5 Z" fill="#000000" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-8"><g><path d="M 70 220 L 70.03 260.03 L 133.63 260" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="stroke" style="stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/><path d="M 138.88 260 L 131.88 263.5 L 133.63 260 L 131.88 256.5 Z" fill="#000000" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Device- Level Security</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="70" y="160" fill="#333333" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">Device- Level Sec...</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-14"><g><path d="M 320 260.03 L 345 260.03 L 345 355 L 363.63 355" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="stroke" style="stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"/><path d="M 368.88 355 L 361.88 358.5 L 363.63 355 L 361.88 351.5 Z" fill="#000000" stroke="#000000" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: rgb(255, 255, 255);"/><rect x="240" y="247.62" width="0" height="0" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" pointer-events="all" style="stroke: rgb(255, 255, 255);"/><path d="M 261.99 256.74 L 261.2 257.26 L 252.04 257.26 L 250.99 258.3 L 248.38 257.26 L 253.61 255.7 L 252.56 256.74 Z M 269.32 252.04 L 268.53 252.83 L 259.37 252.83 L 258.32 253.61 L 255.71 252.57 L 260.94 251.26 L 259.89 252.04 Z M 263.56 255.17 L 264.34 254.66 L 273.77 254.66 L 274.55 253.61 L 277.17 254.66 L 272.2 256.22 L 272.98 255.17 Z M 268.53 250.48 L 269.32 249.97 L 278.48 249.97 L 279.52 248.92 L 282.15 250.22 L 276.91 251.53 L 277.95 250.48 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="none" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(255, 255, 255);"/></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-10"><g><rect x="180" y="243.01" width="50" height="33.97" fill="none" stroke="none" pointer-events="all"/><path d="M 230 252.87 C 230 258.3 218.85 262.71 204.99 262.71 C 191.15 262.71 180 258.3 180 252.87 C 180 267.13 180 267.13 180 267.13 C 180 272.57 191.15 276.98 204.99 276.98 C 218.85 276.98 230 272.57 230 267.13 Z M 204.99 262.71 C 218.85 262.71 230 258.3 230 252.87 C 230 247.42 218.85 243.01 204.99 243.01 C 191.15 243.01 180 247.42 180 252.87 C 180 258.3 191.15 262.71 204.99 262.71 Z" fill="#036897" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(3, 104, 151); stroke: rgb(255, 255, 255);"/><path d="M 168.93 243.99 L 168.93 237.34 M 168.93 257.66 L 168.93 251.02 M 168.93 270.94 L 168.93 264.29 M 171.64 267.81 L 162.37 278.36 M 171.64 261.18 L 162.37 271.33 M 171.64 254.53 L 162.37 264.29 M 171.64 247.89 L 162.37 257.66 M 171.64 241.64 L 162.37 250.63 M 162.37 243.6 L 171.64 235 M 162.37 278.36 L 150 278.36 M 162.37 271.33 L 150 271.33 M 162.37 264.29 L 150 264.29 M 162.37 257.26 L 150 257.26 M 162.37 250.63 L 150 250.63 M 165.84 281.09 L 165.84 274.45 M 165.84 267.81 L 165.84 261.18 M 165.84 254.15 L 165.84 247.5" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-miterlimit="10" pointer-events="all" style="stroke: rgb(255, 255, 255);"/></g></g><g data-cell-id="ZFXfHEiPBkXFW2Xpp-Xw-12"><g><rect x="140" y="190" width="180" height="30" fill="#f5f5f5" stroke="#666666" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(245, 245, 245); stroke: rgb(102, 102, 102);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 178px; height: 1px; padding-top: 205px; margin-left: 141px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #333333; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">Device Role and Location</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="230" y="210" fill="#333333" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">Device Role and Locati...</text></switch></g></g></g></g></g></g><switch><g requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"/><a transform="translate(0,-5)" xlink:href="https://www.drawio.com/doc/faq/svg-export-text-problems" target="_blank"><text text-anchor="middle" font-size="10px" x="50%" y="100%">Text is not SVG - cannot display</text></a></switch></svg>
</div>
```

Influencing Factors on Device-Level Security
:::

### Layer 2 Security Considerations

Securing Layer 2 or the data link layer is as critical as securing higher layers. A highly secure network at the higher layers can be easily taken out of service by simple Layer 2 flooding across its switched network. Therefore, it is critical to secure the network in a bottom-up manner to achieve a reliable and self-healing network security design.

Layer 2 is the simplest with regard to security considerations and one of the most dangerous at the same time. If a hacker compromises a data center switch, they can sniff all traffic passing through it, flood the TCAM table, or send malicious Layer 2 traffic storms. IPv6 also raises a number of first-hop security (FHS) concerns not present in IPv4.

Layer 2 security can be categorized as follows:

- **Authentication and authorization**: Only authorized devices must be permitted to connect to the network at the switch port level. This can be achieved using IEEE 802.1X (which can integrate with a NAC system), port ACLs, dynamic ARP inspection (DAI), DHCP snooping, IP device tracking (IPDT), and other port security features. IPv6 RA guard, DHCPv6 guard, and IPv6 snooping help mitigate IPv6 FHS concerns. IEEE 802.1AE (MACsec) can authenticate and encrypt packets between MACsec-capable devices, such as at DCI edges of interconnected data centers.
- **VLAN design**: Layer 2 domains should be contained to a limited range within the network to create smaller failure domains and avoid ARP or unicast flooding. Private VLANs (PVLANs) offer a more advanced level of controlled traffic separation between VLANs and between endpoints within the same VLAN. VTP should be secured or set to transparent mode to avoid the risk of a rogue switch rewriting VLANs across the entire switched network.
- **Layer 2 control plane**: STP provides protective mechanisms against Layer 2 loops but is a double-edged sword — with no reliable authentication built in, an attacker can attach a switch with better attributes to become the root bridge, causing instability and potential traffic black-holing. Protecting the root bridge by denying rogue switches from participating and filtering BPDU messages at the network edge is essential.

### Layer 3 Control Plane Security Considerations

The Layer 3 control plane steers traffic toward its intended destination. Any wrong information can lead to traffic black-holing and packet drops. In a network with unprotected routing, anyone can bring it down by flooding prefixes, inserting a rogue router, or redirecting traffic to a black-holing next hop. The following are general rules for Layer 3 control plane security:

- Secure the control plane against rogue Layer 3 peers by authenticating routing sessions. This is strongly recommended when peering with an external routing domain.
- Limit the maximum number of routes accepted by a node to prevent memory exhaustion and CPU overload. This is common in MPLS L3VPN SP design, where PE nodes limit the maximum number of prefixes per VRF/VPN.
- Accept only the routes that are expected to be received. ISPs, for example, will only permit the PA or PI address space the customer has agreed to advertise with the agreed subnet length, sometimes combined with uRPF checks.
- Advanced BGP techniques such as QoS policy propagation with BGP (QPPB) and remote black-holing trigger can be used in SP-style networks to offer a more dynamic, flexible, and secure control plane design.
- QoS can guarantee a minimum amount of bandwidth during congestion, ensuring routing sessions are not torn down, and can limit bandwidth to a predefined maximum to protect against DoS attacks from rogue routers. This should ideally be applied across the entire routing domain.
- CoPP should be considered on every network node to relax the impact of control plane flooding and protect network nodes from CPU spikes.

::: {.callout-note}
Although CoPP uses modular QoS CLI (MQoC), QoS provides network-wide per-hop treatment for Layer 3 control plane traffic flows, whereas CoPP is focused only at the device level. Marking down the DSCP value of out-of-profile packets can impact how those packets are treated by other nodes across the network.
:::

### Remote-Access and Network Overlays (VPN) Security Considerations

The most common and proven mechanism for providing secure remote access over the public Internet is the overlaid virtual private network (VPN). One common misconception is that VPN is always synonymous with IPsec. In fact, IPsec offers cryptography at the network layer, whereas VPN can take different forms such as MPLS L3VPN, MPLS L2VPN, SSL VPN, and DMVPN. Secured VPN with IPsec is the most common and proven combination used to protect the various types of VPN solutions.

| | IPsec | GRE | DMVPN | Remote Access (Client Based) |
|:--|:--|:--|:--|:--|
| Standard | Yes | Yes | No (Cisco proprietary) | Yes |
| Overlay transport | P2P IPsec tunnel | P2P GRE tunnel | mGRE tunnel | P2P IPsec |
| Protection model | Peer to peer with IPsec | Peer to peer | Peer to peer with IPsec | Peer to peer with IPsec |
| Dynamic routing | Tunneled | Standard IP WAN | Tunneled | Tunneled |
| Supports NATing | Yes | Yes, with IPsec | Yes, with IPsec | Yes |
| Offers protected VPN over public Internet | Yes | Yes, with IPsec | Yes, with IPsec | Yes* |
| Requires additional network equipment | No | No | No | No |

: Overlay VPN Solutions Comparison {#tbl-vpn-comparison tbl-colwidths="[22,16,16,20,26]"}

\* This VPN is commonly used to provide connectivity over the Internet.

::: {#fig-vpn-overhead}
```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center">
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:411px;" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" version="1.1" width="791px" viewBox="-0.5 -0.5 791 411"><defs/><g><g data-cell-id="0"><g data-cell-id="1"><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-1"><g><rect x="410" y="0" width="120" height="30" fill="#eeeeee" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(238, 238, 238); stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 411px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IP Payload</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="470" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">IP Payload</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-2"><g><rect x="340" y="0" width="70" height="30" fill="#f9f7ed" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(249, 247, 237); stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 68px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 341px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IP HDR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="375" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">IP HDR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-3"><g><rect x="410" y="60" width="120" height="30" fill="#eeeeee" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(238, 238, 238); stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 75px; margin-left: 411px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 68px; height: 1px; padding-top: 315px; margin-left: 201px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">ESP HDR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="235" y="320" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">ESP HDR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-26"><g><rect x="0" y="380" width="100" height="30" fill="#60a917" stroke="#2d7600" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(96, 169, 23); stroke: rgb(45, 118, 0);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 98px; height: 1px; padding-top: 395px; margin-left: 1px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #ffffff; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: #ffffff; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">New IP HDR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="50" y="400" fill="#ffffff" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">New IP HDR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-27"><g><rect x="530" y="300" width="70" height="30" fill="#ffcccc" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(255, 204, 204); stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 68px; height: 1px; padding-top: 315px; margin-left: 531px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">ESP TLR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="565" y="320" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">ESP TLR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-28"><g><rect x="600" y="300" width="70" height="30" fill="#ffcccc" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(255, 204, 204); stroke: rgb(54, 57, 61);"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; text-align: left;" pointer-events="none" width="100%" height="100%" requiredFeatures="http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/feature#Extensibility"><div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="display: flex; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">ESP Auth</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="635" y="320" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">ESP Auth</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-29"><g><rect x="100" y="280" width="240" height="20" fill="#76608a" stroke="#432d57" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(118, 96, 138); "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: #ffffff; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IPsec Transport Mode</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="220" y="295" fill="#ffffff" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">IPsec Transport Mode</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-30"><g><rect x="410" y="380" width="120" height="30" fill="#eeeeee" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(238, 238, 238); "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IP Payload</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="470" y="400" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">IP Payload</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-31"><g><rect x="340" y="380" width="70" height="30" fill="#f9f7ed" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(249, 247, 237); "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IP HDR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="375" y="400" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">IP HDR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-32"><g><rect x="100" y="380" width="70" height="30" fill="#ffcccc" stroke="#36393d" pointer-events="all" style="fill: rgb(255, 204, 204); "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">GRE IP HDR</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="220" y="400" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" text-anchor="middle">GRE IP HDR</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-46"><g><rect x="690" y="0" width="80" height="30" fill="none" stroke="none" pointer-events="all"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 78px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 692px;"><div style="box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000; "><div style="display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Garamond; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal; ">IP Packet</div></div></div></foreignObject><text x="692" y="20" fill="light-dark(#000000, #ffffff)" font-family="Garamond" font-size="16px" font-weight="bold">IP Packet</text></switch></g></g></g><g data-cell-id="yJ7zcC_V2h5qmWiSxmW8-54"><g><rect x="690" y="60" width="80" height="30" fill="none" stroke="none" pointer-events="all"/></g><g><g><switch><foreignObject style="overflow: visible;
```
